Name: PV Row Dimensions and Spacing

Caption.String: Number of modules along side of row
Number of modules along bottom of row

Row spacing is the distance between the bottoms of two rows of modules in the subarray (see diagram above). You can specify row spacing either in meters or as the ground coverage ratio (GCR), where GCR = Length of Side / Row Spacing. SAM uses row spacing for backtracking, self-shading, and land area calculations.

Specify the number of modules along the side and bottom of each row in the subarray so that the product of the modules along side, modules along bottom, and number of rows is equal to the number of modules in the subarray.

Row dimension and spacing inputs are for modules arranged in parallel rectangular rows on flat ground or a flat roof. These inputs are disabled unless self shading, snow losses, or bifacial modules are enabled. For modules in the same plane, such as on a residential rooftop, disable self shading on the Shading and Snow page.

subarray1_nmodx.Label: Number of modules along bottom of row 1

subarray1_nmody.Label: Number of modules along side of row 1

subarray2_nmodx.Label: Number of modules along bottom of row 2

subarray2_nmody.Label: Number of modules along side of row 2

subarray3_nmodx.Label: Number of modules along bottom of row 3

subarray3_nmody.Label: Number of modules along side of row 3

subarray4_nmodx.Label: Number of modules along bottom of row 4

subarray4_nmody.Label: Number of modules along side of row 4

enable(prefix + 'nmodx', (en1 && en2 && en3) || en4 || en5 || en6);
enable(prefix + 'nmody', (en1 && en2 && en3) || en4 || en5 || en6);
